page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 1000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong multi-core and single-core performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory testing. Multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression benchmarks all rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks show strong throughput, while LLM inference speeds for text generation using small and medium models reach top-tier levels. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform in the middle 50% tier.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective alternative for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated cores. The combination of 32 vCPUs, a 1000 GB NVMe SSD, and 64.0 GB of RAM makes it suitable for multi-threaded data processing, text decompression, Redis caching, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 32 shared vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM. Equipped with 1000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and no GPU, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance alongside strong multi-core capabilities. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and text generation for small and medium LLM models, while database operations and static web serving align with average tiers. The processor features a 32 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, Redis caching, and text-generation workloads. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective option for resource-dense workloads that do not require dedicated physical cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1000 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: An AMD EPYC compute instance featuring 32 shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and high-speed NVMe storage for multi-threaded data workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared vCPUs on AMD EPYC x86_64 architecture
64.0 GB of system memory and 1000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Strong multi-threaded decompression and LLM text generation speeds
Suitable for Redis caching, data processing, and text-generation workloads
